rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Demurrer To Complaint, Moving Parties Krista M. Brugnara Individually And As Trustee Of Victoria L. Brugnara Trust, Eric J Brugnara, Kurt D. Huysentruyt. (Added To Calendar For Tentative Ruling Entry Purposes Only)
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 2] In this matter, the defects identified in the Demurrer are potentially correctable through an amended pleading. The Opposition papers contain factual allegations that may address the grounds for demurrer, had they been pleaded in the Complaint. Plaintiff has shown a reasonable possibility exists that amendment could cure the defects. Under settled law, he must be given an opportunity to amend his pleadings with these additional necessary factual allegations. Any amended pleading must be filed within ten (10) days of service of Notice of Entry of Order. The Court agrees with Defendants that the Complaint presents issues and claims that should be heard in the Probate Division of this court. Accordingly, if Plaintiff Luke D. Brugnara choses to file an amended pleading, he is ordered to file the amended pleading in the form of a petition pursuant to the applicable portions of the Probate Code. The Clerk of the Court is directed to assign a PTR- prefix to case number CGC-25-625177, when filing the amended pleading. Defendants shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Demurrer To Complaint, Moving Parties Krista M. Brugnara Individually And As Trustee Of Victoria L. Brugnara Trust, Eric J Brugnara, Kurt D. Huysentruyt.
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 2] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Before the court is Defendants Krista M. Brugnara's and Eric J. Brugnara's Demurrer to Complaint of Luke D. Brugnara. The Court has reviewed the pleadings and makes the following rulings. Defendants' Request for Judicial Notice under the provisions of Evidence Code sections 452, 453, and 454 is GRANTED, as follows. "Judicial notice" is the court's recognition of the existence of a matter of law or fact, without the necessity of formal proof of the existence of a matter of law or fact. (See Evid. C. section 450 et seq.) Judicial notice is limited to the relevant facts properly subject to notice given the materials presented and does not, for example, extend to the truth of assertions in those materials. (See Richtek USA, Inc. v. UPI Semiconductor Corp. (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 651, 659-660.) "A court may take judicial notice of the existence of each document in a court file, but can only take judicial notice of the truth of facts asserted in documents such as orders, findings of fact and conclusions of law, and judgments. [citation.] [ ] A mistaken notion exists that taking judicial notice of court records means noticing " '... the existence of facts asserted in [all the documents in] a court file ... a court cannot take judicial notice of hearsay allegations as being true, just because they are part of a court record or file....' " (Garcia v. Sterling (1985) 176 Cal. App. 3d 17, 21-22, quoting Day v. Sharp (1975) 50 Cal.App.3d 904, 914, emphasis in original.) Demurrer to Complaint of Luke D. Brugnara is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. A demurrer is a pleading used to challenge the legal sufficiency of an opponent's pleading, based on defects that appear either on the face of the pleading under attack or from matters outside the pleading that are judicially noticeable. (See Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, 318; County of Fresno v. Shelton (1998) 66 Cal.App.4th 996, 1008, 1009.) When considering a demurrer, the trial court must accept as true all material facts pleaded in the complaint and those arising by reasonable implication. (Moore v. Conliffe (1994) 7 Cal. 4th 634, 638; Montclair Parkowners Ass'n v. City of Montclair (1999) 76 Cal. App. 4th 784.; Sheehan v. San Francisco 49ers, Ltd. (2009) Cal.4th 992, 998.) A ruling on a demurrer does not determine contested factual issues relating to the merits. Defendants demur on the grounds that all causes of action in the Complaint are inextricably linked to Plaintiff's claims and allegations seeking invalidation of the Victoria L. Brugnara Trust, and are thus time-barred by the Probate Code section 16061.8 statute of limitations. The Complaint does not facially allege facts regarding notice under the Probate Code, or plead around a potential statutory time bar. The Complaint alleges Plaintiff did not receive a copy of the Trust instrument (Complaint par. 18). Further, Plaintiff states in the Opposition to the Demurrer, "[U]ntil Plaintiff read the demurrer he was not aware that any notices pursuant to Probate Code section 16060.5 and 16061.7 had been sent out, let alone received as Defendants claim[.]" (Opp. at 4:24-26.)
